steps.title: Set up the cost formula
steps.content:
The first day costs \$52, and each additional day costs \$26. For $d$ days, there is 1 first day and $(d-1)$ additional days, so the total cost is:
$$C(d) = 52 + 26(d-1)$$

steps.title: Expand and simplify to match a choice
steps.content:
Distribute the 26:
$$C(d) = 52 + 26d - 26$$
Combine like terms:
$$C(d) = 26d + 26$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
